etymology.description: 南(南) (nán, "south") + 鑼(鑼) (luó, "gong") + 鼓(鼓) (gǔ, "drum") + 巷(巷) (xiàng, "alley")
etymology.interpretation: The name 南鑼鼓巷 (Nánluógǔ Xiàng, "South Gong and Drum Alley") reflects its historical location oriented to the south of an area once famous for craftsmen making gongs and drums. These instruments suggest a bustling, lively atmosphere, which is a trait of the neighborhood as a social and cultural hub. The combination evokes an image of an alleyway resonating with musical sounds from gongs and drums, highlighting its vibrant historical significance.

examples.chinese: 南锣鼓巷(南鑼鼓巷)是北京著名的旅游景点。
examples.pinyin: Nán luó gǔ xiàng shì Běijīng zhùmíng de lǚyóu jǐngdiǎn.
examples.english: Nanluoguxiang is a famous tourist attraction in Beijing.

examples.chinese: 每年有很多游客参观南锣鼓巷(每年有很多遊客參觀南鑼鼓巷)。
examples.pinyin: Měinián yǒu hěn duō yóukè cānguān Nán luó gǔ xiàng.
examples.english: Every year, many tourists visit Nanluoguxiang.

examples.chinese: 南锣鼓巷(南鑼鼓巷)有许多历史悠久的建筑和现代化的商店。
examples.pinyin: Nán luó gǔ xiàng yǒu xǔduō lìshǐ yōujiǔ de jiànzhù hé xiàndàihuà de shāngdiàn.
examples.english: Nanluoguxiang has many historic buildings and modern shops.
